title: carmichaelia divaricata
intro: Carmichaelia divaricata is a type of flowering plant native to New Zealand, also known as the spreading broom or broom broom. It is a shrub that belongs to the legume family and has been used by the indigenous Māori people for its medicinal properties. The plant contains flavonoids and alkaloids, which have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and may also help to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Carmichaelia divaricata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and fever.
